124.evaluation_reason: The user asks which reminders exist today; this is a reminder query, not a create/update/cancel/complete request.

125.evaluation_reason: The user asks which reminders exist today, despite the bad-case prefix; this is a reminder query, not a create/update/cancel/complete request.

130.evaluation_reason: The user asks for a reminder five minutes before an end time, but the end time is missing; the agent should clarify instead of creating a reminder.

133.evaluation_reason: The earlier class times are background schedule; the actionable reminder request is only the 19:40 reminder to start writing and reading.

136.evaluation_reason: The request gives a date and broad day period but no concrete clock time; without a product default time, the agent should clarify instead of creating a reminder.

139.evaluation_reason: The user complains that a prior reminder did not fire; this is a reminder status/diagnostic query, not a new create request.

145.evaluation_reason: The user discusses alarm/message wake-up difficulty without requesting a new reminder or giving actionable reminder details.

146.evaluation_reason: The user states a desired wake-up/study plan but does not explicitly request a reminder; the agent should not create one without confirmation.

149.evaluation_reason: The user asks the agent to decide how often to encourage them, but gives no concrete reminder time or interval; the agent should clarify instead of creating a reminder.

150.evaluation_reason: The user asks the agent to recommend how often to remind them, but has not confirmed a concrete cadence; the agent should ask for cadence confirmation instead of creating a reminder.

158.evaluation_reason: The user asks to stop evening wake-up/call reminders without a precise title. A delete attempt with a visible no-match result or a clarification question about the cancellation target are both safe; creating a reminder is not.

161.evaluation_reason: The user asks about being reminded of study progress but gives no concrete reminder time or cadence; the agent should ask for the missing trigger information instead of inferring one from history.

168.evaluation_reason: The user asks not to be disturbed after ending study without a precise reminder target. A delete attempt with a visible no-match result or a clarification question about the cancellation target are both safe; creating a reminder or asking for create time/content is not.

176.evaluation_reason: The user describes a daily study time block but does not explicitly ask to be reminded or supervised; the agent should not create reminders from the schedule statement alone.

180.evaluation_reason: The user is correcting an earlier reminder time. In isolated evaluation there may be no existing 15:50 reminder, so an update attempt with a visible no-match result or a clarification is safe; creating a new reminder would conflate correction with creation.

181.evaluation_reason: The user clarifies a broad evening time range for reminders but gives no reminder content and no exact trigger point; the agent should ask for the missing reminder details instead of creating a reminder.

187.evaluation_reason: In this supervision corpus, an hourly check-in request with an end time is an actionable reminder cadence even when it uses 打卡 rather than the word 提醒.

189.evaluation_reason: The user asks to stop being reminded that it is time to go. A delete attempt with a visible no-match result or a clarification question about the cancellation target are both safe; creating a new reminder is not.

192.evaluation_reason: The user states an intention to wake up before a deadline but does not ask to be reminded, notified, called, or supervised. This is a plan statement, not an executable reminder create request.

197.evaluation_reason: The user asks the agent to remember today's task version and start hourly check-ins until 20:00; this is an actionable reminder/check-in request, not ordinary schedule discussion.

198.evaluation_reason: The user asks to be reminded to stay focused within a time window using an unspecified cadence (随时). Without a concrete frequency or trigger, the agent should clarify the cadence instead of creating one default reminder or inferring multiple reminders.

199.evaluation_reason: The standalone English phrase declines reminders or opts out of reminder help without naming a specific existing reminder to cancel. In isolated evaluation it should not create a reminder or force a CRUD operation.

200.evaluation_reason: The user asks for an every-two-days reminder but gives no concrete time of day. Without an explicit default-time product policy, the agent should ask for the reminder time instead of creating a recurring reminder at an invented time.

205.evaluation_reason: The user asks for an hourly recurring reminder with content but gives no start time or bounded window. Without an explicit default-start policy, the safe behavior is to clarify the start point instead of creating a recurrence from an invented time.

208.evaluation_reason: The user asks to be reminded for a day schedule expressed as time ranges. The ranges provide task boundaries, but the requested reminder trigger point is ambiguous (start, end, or cadence), so the agent should clarify instead of choosing range endpoints by default.

212.evaluation_reason: The user gives a reminder date before a deadline but no clock time on that date. Without a product default-time policy, the agent should ask what time on the 17th instead of creating a midnight reminder.

213.evaluation_reason: The user asks to be reminded for tasks expressed as time ranges and refers to 'these time points'. The ranges are task boundaries, not concrete reminder trigger points, so the agent should clarify frequency or exact reminder times instead of choosing range endpoints.

215.evaluation_reason: The user states a plan start time and task but does not explicitly ask to create a reminder. The agent should confirm reminder intent instead of creating one silently.

219.evaluation_reason: Date-only reminder request gives tomorrow and content but no clock time. Without a product default-time policy, the agent should ask what time tomorrow instead of creating a reminder.

221.evaluation_reason: The user asks what time tomorrow's reminder is. This is a query about existing reminders, not a create request.

222.evaluation_reason: The user asks how an existing reminder at a named time was set. This is a reminder status/detail query, not a new create request.

223.evaluation_reason: The user asks for a daily recurring reminder with content but gives no time of day. Without a default-time policy, the agent should ask what time each day.

225.evaluation_reason: Weekday-only reminder request gives a day and content but no clock time. Without a default-time policy, the agent should ask what time on that day.

231.evaluation_reason: The user thanks or references an alarm without asking to create, modify, query, or cancel a reminder. This should not force reminder CRUD.

238.evaluation_reason: The user asks to be reminded at an unspecified ending time. Reminder intent is present, but the trigger time is missing, so the agent should ask for the concrete time.

245.evaluation_reason: Reminder intent is present, but both the trigger time and reminder content are missing. The agent should ask for the missing details instead of creating a reminder.

247.evaluation_reason: The user complains that an expected daily reminder did not happen. This is a reminder status/diagnostic query, not a new create request.

251.evaluation_reason: The user asks for a reminder 'later today' without a concrete clock time. The agent should ask for the exact time instead of inventing one.

254.evaluation_reason: The user questions whether an existing reminder should happen at this time. This is a reminder status/diagnostic query, not a new create request.

257.evaluation_reason: The user describes a short rest and routine-rest plan without asking to be reminded or supervised. The agent may acknowledge or ask whether a reminder is wanted, but should not promise or create one.

258.evaluation_reason: The user asks to be reminded 'after a while' without a concrete trigger time and without reminder content. The agent should ask for the missing details instead of creating a reminder.

262.evaluation_reason: The user gives a calendar date and broad day period but no concrete clock time. Without a default-time policy, the agent should ask what time that morning instead of creating a reminder.

278.evaluation_reason: The user asks about being woken tomorrow morning but gives no concrete clock time. The agent should ask what time instead of creating a date/daypart default reminder.

279.evaluation_reason: The user complains that an expected reminder did not happen today. This is a reminder status/diagnostic query, not a new create request.

280.evaluation_reason: The user discusses how reminder delivery/acknowledgement works without asking to create, query, update, or cancel a concrete reminder.

287.evaluation_reason: The user complains that an expected 7:00 reminder did not start. This is a reminder status/diagnostic query, not a new create request.

288.evaluation_reason: The user asks to be reminded later today but gives no concrete clock time. The agent should ask for the exact time instead of inventing one.

292.evaluation_reason: The user agrees to a future testing plan where reminder functionality is a task topic, not a request to create a reminder. The agent may discuss the plan but should not create or promise a reminder.

294.evaluation_reason: The user asks which reminders exist today. This is a reminder list query, not a new create request.

300.evaluation_reason: The user states a future plan with a time but does not ask to be reminded. The agent should ask whether a reminder is wanted instead of silently creating one.

304.evaluation_reason: The user asks to be reminded after finishing articles but gives no concrete trigger time or cadence. The agent should ask when to remind instead of creating an unscheduled reminder.

305.evaluation_reason: The user explicitly asks for a reminder but gives content only and no concrete trigger time. The agent should ask when to remind instead of creating an unscheduled reminder.

306.evaluation_reason: The user gives an approximate reminder time but no reminder content. The agent should ask what to remind about instead of creating a reminder with an empty or invented title.

309.evaluation_reason: The user asks to be reminded after an unspecified while and does not provide concrete reminder content. The agent should ask for the missing trigger and content instead of creating an unscheduled reminder.

313.evaluation_reason: The user explicitly asks for a nightly 22:30 reminder to wash up; this is a concrete recurring daily create request.

314.evaluation_reason: The user states a tentative plan to exercise tomorrow but does not ask to be reminded. The agent may discuss the plan or ask whether a reminder is wanted, but should not imply a reminder will happen.

316.evaluation_reason: The user states they are not up at 8:30 but does not ask to create, query, update, cancel, or complete a reminder.

331.evaluation_reason: Date-only reminder request gives next Monday and content but no clock time. Without a product default-time policy, the agent should ask what time on that day instead of creating a reminder.

333.evaluation_reason: The corpus turn is in the late afternoon, so bare '4:37' is expected as 16:37 for the same upcoming occurrence.

335.evaluation_reason: The user asks how the agent knows about a 7 PM departure. This references reminder-like content but does not ask to create, update, cancel, complete, or list reminders.

359.evaluation_reason: The user states a time-bounded study plan but does not explicitly ask for a reminder; the agent should ask whether a reminder is wanted instead of promising an uncreated reminder.

364.evaluation_reason: The user reports that they just started studying at a past clock time. This is a status update, not a request to create a reminder.

371.evaluation_reason: Date-only reminder request gives next Monday and content but no clock time. Without a product default-time policy, the agent should ask what time on that day instead of creating a reminder.

374.evaluation_reason: The user asks to end today's reminders without identifying which reminder or whether all today's reminders should be cancelled. The safe behavior is to ask for the target instead of creating a new reminder.

377.evaluation_reason: The user shares a detailed plan and asks for occasional supervision reminders, plus sleep-prep reminders, but does not clearly specify which plan items should become reminders or the cadence for occasional reminders. The safe behavior is to ask which reminders to set instead of creating every schedule item.

380.evaluation_reason: The user asks whether an alarm/reminder has been set. This is a reminder list/status query, not a request to create a new reminder.

388.evaluation_reason: The user asks to be reminded before a meeting but does not specify how far in advance. Without a default lead-time policy, the agent should ask for the lead time instead of creating the reminder.

389.evaluation_reason: The user provides only lead times for advance reminders, but not the event or base event time to count back from. The agent should ask for the missing event time instead of creating reminders.

390.evaluation_reason: The user provides a detailed time-blocked task list but does not explicitly request reminders. The agent should ask which tasks to remind about instead of creating reminders for every schedule item.

391.evaluation_reason: The isolated corpus case asks to remind about study-related tasks but does not provide the task schedule or concrete times in the current turn. The agent should ask for timing details instead of creating reminders.

394.evaluation_reason: The user states a study plan with a clock time but does not explicitly ask for a reminder. The agent should ask whether to set a reminder instead of creating one.

404.evaluation_reason: The isolated corpus case corrects the reminder timezone to Beijing time but does not include the reminder content or target time in the current turn. The agent should ask for the missing details instead of creating a reminder.

406.evaluation_reason: The user provides a detailed time-blocked daily schedule but does not explicitly ask for reminders. The agent should ask which time points to remind about instead of creating reminders for the whole schedule.

411.evaluation_reason: The user states a future plan with concrete times but does not explicitly ask for a reminder. The agent should ask whether to remind at those times instead of creating reminders.

417.evaluation_reason: The phrase '下个整点再叫我' is an explicit reminder request anchored to the next whole hour, so a one-shot reminder for drawing is expected. The exact local time is run-time dependent.

423.evaluation_reason: The user asks for a reminder during the daytime but does not provide a concrete reminder time or cadence. The agent should ask for the specific time instead of creating a reminder.

425.evaluation_reason: The user provides a detailed time-blocked daily plan but does not explicitly ask for reminders. The agent should ask which time points to remind about instead of creating reminders for the whole schedule.

433.evaluation_reason: The user gives a nickname preference and a conditional supervision style request without a concrete reminder time, cadence, or observable trigger. This should not create a reminder.

436.evaluation_reason: The user states a workout time range and current status but does not explicitly ask for a reminder. The agent should ask whether and when to remind instead of creating one from the range or duration.

437.evaluation_reason: The user references a planned 3 PM drawing start but does not explicitly ask for a reminder. The agent should ask whether to remind instead of creating a reminder from the plan statement.
